rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MAABA VS. DIGNITY HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Set for the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL MAABA'S Motion To Dismiss. Plaintiff Michael Maaba's unopposed Motion To Dismiss is GRANTED. Good cause appearing and a proper showing having been made under California Rules of Court, rule 3.770, the court orders this action and all claims herein dismissed without prejudice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MONACA LAUREEN SHANKS VS. SONESTA INTERNATIONAL HOTELS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Independent Medical Examination
rulings.Rulings: Set for the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Tuesday, May 20, 2025, Line 11. DEFENDANT HEMANT PATEL'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Independent Medical Examination. Defendant Hemant Patel's unopposed Motion To Compel Plaintiff Monica Laureen Shanks' Independent Medical Examination is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Good cause appearing and pursuant to the California Discovery Act, Plaintiff is ordered to submit to an independent medical examination consistent with the specifics set out in Defendant Patel's October 31, 2024, Demand. (See Culley Decl., ex. A; Code of Civil Procedure section 2032,310.) Plaintiff shall submit to the IME forthwith but no later than August 1, 2025. Parties must meet and confer regarding the specific date of the IME. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied on there independent grounds. Defendant failed to identify a specific statutory basis for sanctions under these circumstances. Defendant did not establish an express stipulation for or a court order compelling an IME, so it is not clear it was appropriate prior to this order. Finally, Defendant did not provide a requested amount or any support for the request. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
